title: cli/eval_cmd.py — scripvec eval run
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/eval_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Flags `--queries`, `--judgments`, `--index` default latest, `--format`. Calls `eval.run`. JSON output with metrics table, per-bucket stratified table, ship pass/fail flags, failures_path. `--format text` renders ASCII.

Error map: harness RuntimeError → USER_ERROR `eval_failed`; sanity-probe fail → USER_ERROR `sanity_probe_failed`.

Blocks on: errors, eval.run.

title: eval/run.py — orchestrator + ship criteria
description:
File: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/run.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ShipCriteria` — `hybrid_beats_bm25_recall10`, `dense_beats_bm25_recall10`, `index_size_under_400mb`. Module constants: `HYBRID_VS_BM25_PCT=5`, `DENSE_VS_BM25_PCT=5`, `MAX_INDEX_BYTES=400*1024*1024`.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ModeMetrics` — mode, recall_at_10, recall_at_20, ndcg_at_10, mrr_at_10, latency_p50_ms, latency_p95_ms.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class EvalReport` — index_hash, metrics (tuple of ModeMetrics for bm25/dense/hybrid), recall10_by_bucket (mode → bucket → float), ship, failures_path.
- `run(queries_path, judgments_path, *, index="latest") -> EvalReport` — load + validate, run sanity probes, run every query × every mode, capture latency, compute metrics + stratification, evaluate ship criteria, write `failures_<timestamp>.jsonl`, return EvalReport.
- Private `_compute_ship`, `_write_failures`, `_index_dir_size_bytes`, `FailureRow`.

All failures propagate (ADR-001). Blocks on: metrics, dataset, retrieval.query/bm25/store/paths/manifest.

title: retrieval/config.py — EmbedConfig + env loader
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/config.py`.

Per ADR-005 + CR-001 § Function plan. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class EmbedConfig` — `base_url, api_key, model, dim` with non-empty invariants.
- `load_embed_config() -> EmbedConfig` — env first (`OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, model id, dim), optional non-committed config file second. Never hardcode. Raises `RuntimeError` if any field cannot be resolved (ADR-001).
- Private `_read_optional_config_file() -> dict` — returns empty dict if absent; raises only on corrupt file.

Accept: unit tests for missing env (raises), valid env (returns config), corrupt optional file (raises).

title: retrieval/paths.py — data dir helpers + latest symlink
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/paths.py`.

Implement data-dir layout helpers per CR-001. All other modules ask this one, nobody else joins strings:
- `data_dir()` — honors `SCRIPVEC_DATA_DIR`, default `./data/`.
- `raw_dir()`, `eval_dir()`, `logs_dir()`, `indexes_dir()`, `index_path(hash)`.
- `latest_symlink()`, `resolve_latest() -> str` (raises if missing), `set_latest(hash)` (atomic swap via `os.replace`).
- `index_path` validates hash against `^[0-9a-f]{32}$` regex.

No in-repo imports. No silent fallback to mtime (ADR-001).

title: retrieval/manifest.py + test — index manifest + hash
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/manifest.py` and `test_manifest.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class Manifest` with fields per CR-001 § Function plan: `corpus_source, corpus_commit_sha, tokenizer_version, embed_endpoint, embed_model, embed_dim, embed_normalized, bm25_lib, bm25_lib_version, bm25_k1, bm25_b, vec_schema_version`. ADR-009 chunker params (per-chunk cap, no-split floor) must also be included in the hashed manifest per that ADR; add `nonverse_chunk_cap` and `nonverse_chunk_floor` fields.
- `config_hash(m) -> str` — BLAKE2b-128 over canonical JSON (delegates to hashing.py).
- `write_manifest(m, path)` — atomic write-temp-then-replace.
- `read_manifest(path) -> Manifest` — strict. Missing or extra fields raise (ADR-001).

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: round-trip, missing required → raise, unknown field → raise, type mismatch → raise.

Blocks on: hashing.py.

title: reference/reference.py — full grammar (single, range, list, extraction)
description:
File: `packages/reference/src/scripvec_reference/reference.py`.

Implements ADRs 010, 011, 012, 013 in one file (one-agent ownership avoids same-file contention). Required public surface:

- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class Reference` — book/chapter/verse with the invariants from CR-001 § Function plan.
- `parse_reference(s) -> Reference` — ADR-010 strict single-citation grammar. No abbreviations, case variants, leading zeros, alt punctuation.
- `parse_range(s) -> tuple[Reference, Reference]` — ADR-011 `<ref> - <ref>` (ASCII hyphen, surrounding spaces). Reversed-order raises.
- `parse_list(s) -> list[Reference|Range]` — ADR-012 `;`-separated; dedupe valid duplicates; malformed item names its ordinal in the raise.
- `extract_references(text) -> list[...]` — ADR-013 free-text scan using the single-ref + range grammars (not list). No-candidate case returns empty (not a raise).
- `canonical(book, chapter, verse) -> str` — round-trips with parse_reference.
- Private `_split_book_and_loc` per CR-001.

Blocks on: books.py. Accept: unit tests per ADRs 010-013 (positive + explicit negative cases).

title: eval/dataset.py + test — JSONL loaders + sanity probes
description:
Files: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/dataset.py` and `test_dataset.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001. Implement:
- `QueryRow`, `Judgment`, `SanityProbe` dataclasses with invariants from CR-001 § Function plan.
- `SANITY_PROBES: tuple[SanityProbe, ...]` — module constant. Placeholder values (filled in authoring bead); constant surface must exist now.
- `load_queries(path) -> list[QueryRow]` — JSONL parse; raises on duplicate query text/id, malformed row, <8 per canonical bucket (doctrinal/narrative/phrase-memory/proper-noun).
- `load_judgments(path, known_verse_ids) -> list[Judgment]` — raises on unknown verse_id, grade ∉ {1,2}, malformed row.
- `run_sanity_probes(idx: Bm25Index)` — raises if any probe's expected verses don't appear in BM25 top-3.
- Private `_parse_query_row`, `_parse_judgment_row` (line numbers in raise messages).

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: well-formed accept, duplicate text/id raises, malformed JSON raises (line number), under-8-per-bucket raises, unknown verse_id raises, invalid grade (0 or 3) raises.

Blocks on: bm25.py (for sanity probe runtime).

title: corpus_ingest/verse.py — VerseRecord + verse_id slug
description:
File: `packages/corpus_ingest/src/scripvec_corpus_ingest/verse.py`.

Per CR-001 § File blueprint, design question 1 (resolved 2026-04-20: slug). Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class VerseRecord` — fields `verse_id, ref_canonical, book, chapter, verse, text` with invariants from CR-001.
- `make_verse_id(ref_canonical) -> str` — stable human-readable slug (`1-nephi-3-7`, `dandc-88-118`). Lowercase, replace ` `/`:` with `-`, `&` with `and`. Raises on empty input.

No in-repo imports. Accept: unit test round-trips a few canonical refs.

title: corpus_ingest/chunker.py — ADR-009 non-verse sentence-packer
description:
File: `packages/corpus_ingest/src/scripvec_corpus_ingest/chunker.py`.

Per ADR-009. Handles non-verse canonical text from bcbooks: BoM title page, Testimony of Three/Eight Witnesses, pre-1921 book-level headings, the 15 in-text 1830 chapter headings (Mosiah 9/23; Alma 5/7/9/17/21/36/38/39/45; Helaman 7/13; 3 Nephi 11; Mormon), D&C `signature` field.

Policy (ADR-009):
- Chunk = one or more consecutive sentences.
- Greedy pack to the per-chunk token cap.
- No-split floor: items with token count ≤ floor emitted whole regardless of cap.
- Invariant `floor > cap` asserted at config load — raise if violated.
- **Token counter must be the same counter as `embed.py`'s 8K gate** (shared via embed module's public surface).
- Sentence splitter library choice is local (NLTK punkt / regex — must be deterministic and offline).
- Emit chunk records with same retrieval shape as `VerseRecord` plus metadata (`kind`, source item id, position within source) so retrieval can't tell them apart during RRF.

Chunker params (`nonverse_chunk_cap`, `nonverse_chunk_floor`) read from project-root config file (not stipulated in ADR; file introduced here or follow-on CR). For MVP, accept a transitional code-local placeholder per ADR-009 § Conflicts; document as placeholder.

Blocks on: embed.py (token counter).

title: cli/version_cmd.py — scripvec --version
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/version_cmd.py`.

Per ADR-007 + CR-002. JSON output `{cli_version, embedding_model, latest_index_hash}`. Implement `register(app)` wiring `version` callback + `--version` flag. On missing config or missing latest, routes through `errors.emit_error` with stable code.

Blocks on: config.py, paths.py, errors.py.

title: cli/test_contracts.py — ADR-007 per-subcommand contract tests
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/test_contracts.py`.

ADR-007 validation clause. One success-path and one forced-failure test per subcommand (`query`, `index build`, `index list`, `eval run`, `feedback`, `--version`). Uses Typer's CliRunner; `json.loads` on stdout/stderr.

Specific tests per CR-001 § File blueprint test listings:
- query: documented JSON schema on success; structured stderr on bad flag.
- index build: schema (index_hash, latest); drift → structured stderr.
- index list: array shape with hash/created_at/model/dim/is_latest; unreadable dir → structured stderr.
- eval run: schema (metrics, recall10_by_bucket, ship, failures_path, index_hash); unknown verse_id in judgments → structured stderr code `unknown_verse_id`.
- feedback: confirmation echo; bad grade → structured stderr.
- version: schema (cli_version, embedding_model, latest_index_hash); missing latest → structured stderr.

Blocks on: main.py.

title: cli/query_log.py — JSONL append for queries
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/query_log.py`.

Per CR-002. CLI-boundary concern (not in retrieval — design note in CR-001). Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class QueryLogRecord` — timestamp, schema_version="1", session_id, query_id, index_hash, mode, query, k, results (verse_id + per-component ranks + rrf_score), latency_ms.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ResultLogRow`.
- `append(record)` — atomic JSONL line write to `paths.logs_dir() / "queries.jsonl"`. Creates parents if absent. Raises on OSError (no silent swallow).
- `new_session_id()`, `new_query_id()` — UUID4 hex.

Blocks on: paths.py (only out-of-CLI dep).

title: retrieval/bm25.py — BM25S wrapper
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/bm25.py`.

Per CR-002. Implement:
- `@dataclass class Bm25Index` (opaque; holds BM25S retriever + index-aligned verse_id corpus mapping).
- `build_bm25(verses, index_dir)` — `k1=1.5`, `b=0.75`. Tokenizes via `tokenizer.tokenize`. Persist in BM25S native on-disk format + sidecar `verse_ids.json`. Raises on empty corpus.
- `load_bm25(index_dir) -> Bm25Index` — strict; raises on missing or version mismatch.
- `bm25_topk(idx, query, k=50) -> list[tuple[str, float]]` — tokenize query + score + map to `verse_id`. Tiebreak by `verse_id` ascending (ADR-007).

Unit-untested per CR-001 (integration surface). Blocks on: tokenizer.py, verse.py.

title: retrieval/build.py + test — build_index + corpus-drift guard
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/build.py` and `test_build.py`.

Per CR-002 + CR-001 § Function plan. Implement:
- `build_index(*, from_scratch=True, rebuild_corpus=False) -> str` — full sequence: corpus-drift check → iter_verses + chunker for non-verse text → assemble Manifest → config_hash → serial per-record embed (verse-chunk calls marked for ADR-015 telemetry) → transactional `insert_batch` in `corpus.sqlite` → `build_bm25` → `write_manifest` → `set_latest` only on success. Returns the new config_hash.
- `_assemble_manifest(cfg, commit_sha) -> Manifest`.
- `_drift_check_corpus(stored, observed, rebuild_corpus)` — no-op on from-scratch (no stored commit); else commits must match unless rebuild_corpus=True. Raises naming both commits.
- `from_scratch=False` raises `NotImplementedError` — incremental is out of MVP.
- **Serial embedding invariant (ADR-006)**: plain Python loop; no ThreadPoolExecutor/asyncio.gather. Verified by review.
- **Atomic publish**: `set_latest` only on the final success path.

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: drift-guard passes on match / no stored / rebuild_corpus=True; raises on mismatch with both commits in message.

Blocks on: embed, embed_telemetry, manifest, store, bm25, bcbooks, chunker.

title: cli/main.py — Typer root + subcommand registration
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/main.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Construct `app = typer.Typer(add_completion=False, pretty_exceptions_enable=False, no_args_is_help=True)`. At import, call `register(app)` on each subcommand module (`query_cmd`, `index_cmd`, `eval_cmd`, `feedback_cmd`, `version_cmd`).

Wired to console script `scripvec = "scripvec_cli.main:app"` (already in pyproject.toml). No wrapper function, no __main__.py.

Blocks on: all subcommand modules.

title: cli/feedback_cmd.py — scripvec feedback
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/feedback_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002. Flags `--query-id, --verse-id, --grade {0,1,2}, --note`. Appends one record to `data/logs/feedback.jsonl` (timestamp, schema_version, query_id, verse_id, grade, note), prints JSON confirmation. Bad grade → USER_ERROR via emit_error.

Blocks on: paths.py, errors.py.

title: eval/metrics.py + test — recall@k, nDCG@10, MRR@10, percentile
description:
Files: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/metrics.py` and `test_metrics.py`.

Per CR-002 (0/1/2 graded; absence = 0). Pure functions:
- `recall_at_k(retrieved, relevant, k)`.
- `ndcg_at_10(retrieved, relevant)` — `gain(grade) = 2**grade - 1`.
- `mrr_at_10(retrieved, relevant)`.
- `percentile(samples, p)`.

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded test coverage: known-fixture values, empty-relevant returns 0.0, perfect-order nDCG = 1.0, gain formula (grade-2 → 3, grade-1 → 1), MRR inverse rank, percentile on known samples, ValueErrors on bad args.

title: retrieval/embed_telemetry.py — ADR-015 running average
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/embed_telemetry.py`.

Per ADR-015. The embed client's per-verse-chunk latency instrumentation. Implement:
- Record per-call timing (request initiation → normalized vector returned).
- Maintain a running average scoped per active `(endpoint, model, dim, normalize)` config tuple.
- Append-only per-call timing log under `paths.logs_dir()`; separate running-average state file.
- Config change resets the live average; prior-config average retained for audit.
- Startup re-derive path from the append-only log when the running-average file is missing; raise on corruption (ADR-001).
- **Exception carve-out per ADR-015 §5**: telemetry-write failure logs at warn and does NOT raise (embed call itself must not be penalized). Startup corruption DOES raise.

Blocks on: paths.py. CLI surface to read the live average is wired later via `scripvec stats embed` or equivalent; this bead only produces the storage + update API the embed client calls.

title: retrieval/query.py + test — query path, drift guard, ADR-013/014
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/query.py` and `test_query.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADRs 013/014 + CR-001 § Function plan. Implement:
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class QueryResult` with CR-002 output shape (query, mode, k, index, results tuple, latency_ms).
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class ResultRow` — rank, verse_id, ref, text, score, scores dict; **plus `forced: bool` marker per ADR-014**.
- `query(text, *, k=10, mode="hybrid", index="latest") -> QueryResult`:
  1. `_resolve_index` → (hash, dir).
  2. Load manifest; `_drift_check_endpoint` vs runtime EmbedConfig (ADR-001). Raise on mismatch.
  3. **ADR-013 extraction**: `reference.extract_references(text)` → resolved verse set. Grammatical-but-nonexistent raises.
  4. Dispatch on mode: `_run_bm25`, `_run_dense` (via embed + dense_topk), rrf. Capture per-stage latency.
  5. **ADR-014 force-inclusion**: merge extracted verse set into results; dedupe; mark each row `forced: True|False` (True if came from extraction — both if dedupe collision); default position = top in query-appearance order; default count contract = additive (may return `k + N_extracted`).
  6. Return CR-002 shape; `latency_ms` has bm25/dense/fuse/total keys (0.0 for absent stages — stable schema).
- Private `_resolve_index`, `_drift_check_endpoint`, `_run_bm25`, `_run_dense`.

Tests per CR-001 § Expanded: drift-check on url/model/dim mismatch (names field + both values); resolve latest/explicit-hash; missing directory raises. **Also ADR-014 tests**: force-included also-organically-retrieved dedupes to one row with both markers; force-included-not-organic adds an extra row; no extraction → organic unchanged; range overlap partial.

Blocks on: config, paths, embed, manifest, store, bm25, rrf, reference (extract).

title: Workspace wiring: add runtime deps and sync
description:
Serial; all Sprint 1 work depends on this. Add runtime deps to package pyprojects per CR-001 System 1:
- `packages/retrieval/pyproject.toml`: `httpx`, `sqlite-vec`, `bm25s`, `numpy`.
- `apps/scripvec_cli/pyproject.toml`: `typer`.
- Other packages: verify no deps needed at runtime.

Run `uv sync` at workspace root; confirm a single lockfile resolves. Confirm `data/indexes/`, `data/logs/` gitignored; `data/raw/bcbooks/*.json` and `data/eval/*.jsonl` tracked (ADR-003).

Accept: `uv sync` green; `uv run python -c 'import typer, httpx, sqlite_vec, bm25s, numpy'` succeeds from workspace root.

title: cli/errors.py — ExitCode + emit_error
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/errors.py`.

Per ADR-007 + CR-002. Implement:
- `class ExitCode` with `SUCCESS=0, USER_ERROR=1, NOT_FOUND=2, UPSTREAM_ERROR=3`.
- `emit_error(code, message, details=None, exit_code=USER_ERROR) -> NoReturn` — writes `{"error":{"code","message","details"}}` JSON to stderr, calls `sys.exit`.

No in-repo imports. Single writer so error shape can't drift.

title: data/eval/judgments.jsonl — graded relevance
description:
File: `data/eval/judgments.jsonl` (authored content).

Per CR-002. One JSON object per line: `{query_id, verse_id, grade}` with `grade ∈ {1, 2}` (`0` = absent from file, per ADR).

- Grade 1 = relevant; 2 = canonical/definitive.
- Every `verse_id` must exist in the live corpus.
- Every `query_id` must exist in `queries.jsonl`.

Blocks on: queries.jsonl (needs query_ids) + a built index (needs verse_ids).

title: eval/dataset.py SANITY_PROBES values — fill in constant
description:
Edit: `packages/eval/src/scripvec_eval/dataset.py` — fill in the `SANITY_PROBES` module constant with three `SanityProbe(query=..., expected_top3=(...))` tuples per CR-002. Each probe's expected verse(s) must score in the BM25 top-3.

These are a contract the harness enforces, not data a human edits later (per CR-001 § design note).

Blocks on: queries.jsonl + a built index (need real verse_ids from the live corpus).

title: retrieval/rrf.py + test — Reciprocal Rank Fusion
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/rrf.py` and `test_rrf.py`.

Per CR-002. Pure math:
- `rrf(bm25_hits, dense_hits, *, k=60, top_k=10) -> list[tuple[str, float]]` — `score(d) = Σ 1/(k + rank_i(d))`, ties broken by `verse_id` ascending (ADR-007 deterministic). Raises `ValueError` on `k<1` or `top_k<1`.

Tests: formula on known inputs, tiebreak, top_k truncation, disjoint inputs, ValueError on bad args.

title: retrieval/__init__.py — public re-exports
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/__init__.py`.

Per CR-001 § Module-boundary contracts. Re-export: `build_index`, `query`, `QueryResult`, `ResultRow`. **Do NOT re-export `embed`** (ADR-006, CR-001 § design question 5 resolved hidden).

Blocks on: build.py, query.py.

title: retrieval/store.py — sqlite-vec + verses table
description:
File: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/store.py`.

Per CR-002 persistence. Implement:
- `@dataclass class StoreConn` holding the sqlite3 connection with `sqlite-vec` extension loaded.
- `@dataclass(frozen=True) class DenseHit` — verse_id/rowid/cosine/record.
- `open_store(path, *, create=False) -> StoreConn` — opens or creates `corpus.sqlite` with `verses(rowid, verse_id, ref_canonical, book, chapter, verse, text)` and `vec0 float[dim]` virtual table. Dim read from EmbedConfig, not hardcoded. Raises if extension load fails or on dim drift.
- `insert_batch(conn, rows)` — transactional; verses row + vec0 row in same txn per batch, keyed by same rowid. Fails loud on dim mismatch (ADR-001).
- `dense_topk(conn, query_vec, k=50) -> list[DenseHit]` — inner-product top-k joined to verses in one SQL. Inner product over L2-normalized vecs is cosine (ADR-005).
- `get_verse(conn, verse_id) -> VerseRecord` — raises on missing row.

Unit-untested per CR-001 (integration surface). Blocks on: config.py, verse.py.

title: cli/index_cmd.py — scripvec index build + list
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/index_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Two subcommands under one `index` Typer sub-app:
- `index build` flags `--from-scratch`, `--rebuild-corpus`. Calls `retrieval.build_index`. Prints `{index_hash, latest}` JSON on success.
- `index list` — walks `paths.indexes_dir()/*/config.json`, reads each manifest, prints JSON array of `{hash, created_at, model, dim, is_latest}` sorted by hash asc. `created_at` from file mtime per CR-001 § design question 2.

Error map: drift RuntimeError → USER_ERROR `corpus_drift`; embed → UPSTREAM_ERROR `embedding_endpoint`; else USER_ERROR `build_failed`.

Blocks on: errors, retrieval.build, retrieval.paths, retrieval.manifest.

title: Build and validate — run ship criteria
description:
Per CR-001 § Implementation plan step 10. Final validation bead.

Steps:
1. `scripvec index build --from-scratch` — confirm `data/indexes/<hash>/` contains `corpus.sqlite`, `bm25.bm25s`, `config.json`; confirm `data/indexes/latest` points at it.
2. Smoke: `scripvec query "faith and works" --k 10` — confirm JSON validates against documented schema.
3. `scripvec eval run --queries data/eval/queries.jsonl --judgments data/eval/judgments.jsonl`.
4. Report each ship criterion in eval output:
   a. hybrid beats BM25 by ≥5 pts recall@10.
   b. dense beats BM25 by ≥5 pts recall@10.
   c. index dir ≤400 MB on disk.

Do NOT paper over failures (ADR-001). A failing criterion is the signal — record it in the CR-001 audit log and open follow-up CRs per the project's workflow. Per ADR-005 "response to poor dense performance is not to swap models on the current endpoint" — either switch endpoints or invest in non-model quality axes.

Blocks on: every earlier bead.

title: cli/query_cmd.py — scripvec query
description:
File: `apps/scripvec_cli/src/scripvec_cli/query_cmd.py`.

Per CR-002 + ADR-007. Flags: positional text, `--k 10`, `--mode {bm25|dense|hybrid}` default hybrid, `--format {json|text}` default json, `--index {<hash>|latest}` default latest, `--show-scores`.

Implement `register(app)` + private `_run_query`, `_to_log_record`, `_format_text`. Call `retrieval.query`, format to CR-002 JSON on stdout, append one record to `queries.jsonl`. ADR-007 help text: purpose/flags/schema/exit codes/JSON example.

Error map: ValueError flags → USER_ERROR `bad_flag`; missing index → NOT_FOUND `index_not_found`; drift/embed → UPSTREAM_ERROR `endpoint_drift` or `embedding_endpoint`.

Blocks on: errors, query_log, retrieval.query.

title: retrieval/hashing.py + test — canonical_json + blake2b_128_hex
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/hashing.py` and `test_hashing.py`.

Per CR-001 § File blueprint (Ousterhout split for testability):
- `canonical_json(obj) -> bytes` — sorted keys, no whitespace, UTF-8.
- `blake2b_128_hex(payload: bytes) -> str` — 32-char lowercase hex digest.

Test file per CR-001 § Expanded test coverage: sort-keys invariance, no-whitespace, UTF-8 round trip, 32-char length, determinism, composition invariance.

No in-repo imports. Pure functions.

title: retrieval/tokenizer.py + test — BM25 tokenization
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/tokenizer.py` and `test_tokenizer.py`.

Per CR-002 BM25 rules. Implement:
- `tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]` — pure function: lowercase → Unicode NFC → split on `\W+` → drop empties → drop pure-digit tokens < 2 chars. Stopwords off.

Tests per CR-001: lowercasing, NFC normalization, word-boundary split, drop short digit tokens, keep alphanumerics, determinism.

title: corpus_ingest/bcbooks.py — JSON walker + commit SHA
description:
File: `packages/corpus_ingest/src/scripvec_corpus_ingest/bcbooks.py`.

Per CR-001 § Function plan. Walks `data/raw/bcbooks/{book-of-mormon,doctrine-and-covenants}.json`:
- `iter_verses(data_dir) -> Iterator[VerseRecord]` — yields BoM then D&C, canonical-book order, chapter/verse ascending. Invariants per `VerseRecord`.
- `corpus_commit_sha(data_dir) -> str` — git SHA of `data/raw/bcbooks/`. Raises if not a git repo, no commit touches path (ADR-001).
- Private `_iter_book_of_mormon`, `_iter_doctrine_and_covenants`.

Raises: FileNotFoundError on missing JSON; ValueError on JSON shape mismatch.

Blocks on: verse.py, reference.py (canonicalize_book + canonical).

title: reference/books.py — canonical book table
description:
File: `packages/reference/src/scripvec_reference/books.py`.

Per CR-001 § File blueprint, ADR-010 strict canonical parser. Implement:
- `CANONICAL_BOOKS: tuple[str, ...]` — BoM books in canonical order (1 Nephi → Moroni), then `D&C`.
- `canonicalize_book(raw: str) -> str` — maps every spelling present in `data/raw/bcbooks/*.json` to the pinned canonical form. Case-sensitive per ADR-010. Raises `ValueError` on unknown input (ADR-001).
- Private `_NAME_TABLE: dict[str, str]` lookup.

Accept: unit covering positive (every canonical book), negative (abbrev, case variants per ADR-010's rejection list).

title: data/eval/queries.jsonl — authored 50-query floor
description:
File: `data/eval/queries.jsonl` (authored content, not a module).

Per CR-002 § Evaluation harness + CR-001 System 9. Author 50-query floor (75 target). One JSON object per line: `{query_id, query, tags, notes?}`.

Coverage requirements (enforced by `load_queries`):
- ≥8 queries in each of four canonical buckets: `doctrinal`, `narrative`, `phrase-memory`, `proper-noun`.
- No duplicate `query` strings.
- No duplicate `query_id`s.

Author queries that exercise the named failure modes (CR-002): proper-noun retrieval (Teancum, Coriantumr), phrase-memory ("arise and shine forth"), doctrinal themes, narrative recall.

Blocks on: nothing code-side. Can start once eval.dataset defines the JSONL schema (bead 020).

title: retrieval/embed.py + test — sanctioned embed client
description:
Files: `packages/retrieval/src/scripvec_retrieval/embed.py` and `test_embed.py`.

Per ADRs 005, 006, 001, 015. The single sanctioned entry point to the embedding endpoint. Implement:
- `embed(text: str) -> list[float]` — synchronous, serial, L2-normalized, dim-checked. No async, no batch, no retry.
- `_estimate_token_count(text) -> int` — conservative upper bound (char/3 + word_count).
- `_post_embedding(cfg, text) -> list[float]` — raw POST + parse of `data[0].embedding`.
- `_l2_normalize(vec) -> list[float]` — raises on zero norm.
- **ADR-015 hook**: on successful verse-chunk embed call, update running average via `embed_telemetry` module. Use a marker arg or second public entry point to distinguish verse-chunk calls from query/non-verse calls.

Raises (ADR-005/001): `RuntimeError` on >8K token input, non-2xx HTTP, dim mismatch, malformed response.

Tests (ADR-005 validation): wrong-length response raises with observed+expected dims, oversized input raises without HTTP call, non-2xx raises with status in message, normalized vector returned on success.

Blocks on: config.py.
